vitals.entry_fee: KZT 15,000 through June 30; KZT 20,000 from July 1; KZT 25,000 for foreign participants

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64. FLAG (2026-07-24 editorial wave 7): vitals corrected from 99.8km/1,067m (unsourced) to the organizer-confirmed 95km course; elevation set to null (no official figure found, was invented). altitude=4 and climbing=4 were scored against unverified numbers — recommend a human re-check whether those hold for a 95km lap-course race with no confirmed elevation total. Numbers left untouched per no-silent-reshuffle rule.

course_description.character: A lap-based road course starting and finishing at the Halyk Bank parking area on Al-Farabi Street in Almaty. The organizer runs three linked categories on the same asphalt circuit: a 95 km Gran Fondo and 50 km Media Fondo for road bikes, plus a separate 37 km MTB category. The organizer describes the course as hilly, with steep ascents and smooth descents, rather than a single point-to-point mountain profile — but has not published a route-level elevation total or climb-by-climb breakdown to back that characterization.
course_description.signature_challenge: The repeated hill sequences within the lap circuit — no single named climb dominates, since the organizer markets the course as a hilly asphalt loop rather than a mountain point-to-point.

logistics.transport: Start/finish at the Halyk Bank parking area, 40 Al-Farabi Street, Almaty; 06:00 start.

history.origin_story: First run as an amateur race in late September 2016, per an organizer interview with founder Samat Sapazhanov. Now organized in partnership with the Kazakhstan Cycling Federation and marketed as part of the Gran Fondo World Tour (GFWT) network.

biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Kazakhstan is a genuine, timed road race in Almaty — a 95 km Gran Fondo and 50 km Media Fondo on a timed asphalt lap course, plus a separate 37 km MTB category that doesn't compromise the road event's identity. It has run since 2016 and has real continuity: a 2025 edition with organizer-published results, and a confirmed 2026 date. The catch is the marketing. The organizer calls it the "Gran Fondo World Championship," but its own regulations qualify that as a title "according to the GFWT version" — Gran Fondo World Tour branding, not a UCI World Championship. Read the name literally and you'll overrate what this is.
strengths: Genuinely timed, continuously run road race with a real 2016 founding and organizer-published 2025 results
Clear separation between the 95/50 km road categories and the 37 km MTB option — the road event isn't diluted by mixed-surface entries
Accessible entry fee (KZT 15,000-25,000, roughly $30-50) relative to most international gran fondos
Kazakhstan Cycling Federation involvement gives independent verification beyond the organizer's own site
weaknesses: "Gran Fondo World Championship" branding invites confusion with the UCI Gran Fondo World Championships — it is not that; it's a GFWT-network title

No official elevation-gain figure has ever been published for the 95 km course, despite this database's prior file inventing a 1,067m figure and a Tien Shan/Medeu climbing narrative unsupported by organizer material
No verified field-size data — the 48,000 figure sometimes shown on the homepage is a GFWT network-wide statistic, not the Almaty race's actual attendance
English-language race pages contain untranslated fee text, and the lap-based course format is less destination-scenic than the branding implies
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate, long-running Central Asian road race worth trusting for what it actually is — a well-priced, organized 95 km/50 km timed event — as long as you discount the "World Championship" language and don't expect a documented mountain profile.

final_verdict.one_liner: Legitimate Almaty road race since 2016, oversold as a 'World Championship' it isn't.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want an affordable, genuinely timed road race in Central Asia with real organizational continuity. Don't expect a UCI-sanctioned World Championship field or documented high-mountain climbing — this is a hilly asphalt lap course, not a Tien Shan epic.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a mountain road gran fondo with verified high-altitude climbing, look at established Alpine or Pyrenean GFNY/UCI Gran Fondo World Series events instead.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Kazakhstan Tour of Zhetysu 2019
videos.description:
GRAN FONDO – самый известный и популярный в мире шоссейный вело-заезд, объединяющий на одной трассе как профессиональных спортсменов, так и любителей велоспорта.

Маршрут Gran Fondo Kazakhstan Tour of Zhetysu проложен по самым живописным местам Алматинской области. Холмы, крутые подъемы и гладкие спуски – это уникальная асфальтированная трасса, которая не даст заскучать своим участникам!

rider_intel.search_text: The available transcript from the Gran Fondo Kazakhstan in Almaty provides limited race-specific information. One participant expressed appreciation for the event organization and their experience returning to the race. However, the transcript lacks detailed information about course conditions, terrain challenges, specific mile markers, gear recommendations, or race day logistics that would typically inform riders preparing for this 62-mile gravel event with 3500 feet of elevation gain. More comprehensive video content would be needed to provide actionable intelligence about the course features, surface conditions, key climbs, pacing strategies, or equipment considerations for this Tier 2 gravel race in Kazakhstan.

eligibility.notes: Mass-participation road Gran Fondo in Almaty with registration and schedule published for 16 August 2026.; last edition evidenced: 2026; via sonar-pro eligibility_audit; codex gpt-5.6-sol web research (2026-07-24) confirmed 95km course, Aug 16 start, KZT entry fees, and 2016 founding (not 2022); elevation and field size remain unverified.
